+{- | Render the queued messages management panel.
+This is only shown when the conversation is paused and has queued messages.
+-}
+render_queued_messages_manager :: TuiState -> Conversation -> Widget N
+render_queued_messages_manager st conv =
+    -- Only show queue management when conversation is paused
+    if conversationStatus conv /= ConversationStatus_Paused
+        then emptyWidget
+        else
+            let queuedMsgs = getQueuedMessages st conv
+                count = length queuedMsgs
+             in if count == 0
+                    then emptyWidget
+                    else render_queue_panel st count queuedMsgs
+
+-- | Render the queue management UI panel.
+render_queue_panel :: TuiState -> Int -> [Text] -> Widget N
+render_queue_panel st count msgs =
+    borderWithFocus
+        st
+        QueuedMessageListWidget
+        (" Queued Messages (" <> Text.pack (show count) <> ") ")
+        $ vBox
+            [ txt "Ctrl+D: clear all | Del/Backspace: delete selected | Up/Down: select"
+            , txt ""
+            , render_queued_message_list selectedIdx msgs
+            ]
+  where
+    selectedIdx = st ^. tuiUI . queuedMessagesFocus
+
+-- | Render the list of queued messages with selection.
+render_queued_message_list :: Maybe Int -> [Text] -> Widget N
+render_queued_message_list selectedIdx msgs =
+    vBox $ zipWith (render_queued_item selectedIdx) [0 ..] msgs
+
+-- | Render a single queued message item.
+render_queued_item :: Maybe Int -> Int -> Text -> Widget N
+render_queued_item selectedIdx idx msg =
+    let isSelected = selectedIdx == Just idx
+        marker = if isSelected then "в–¶ " else "  "
+        truncated = Text.take 60 msg <> if Text.length msg > 60 then "..." else ""
+        attr = if isSelected then queuedMessageSelectedAttr else queuedMessageAttr
+     in withAttr attr $ txt $ marker <> truncated
+

+### рҹ’¬ User Query
+
+# TUI: Add message queue management for paused conversations
+
+## Overview
+
+The TUI already has a message buffering mechanism that allows users to queue messages while an agent is processing tool calls. Currently, users can see queued messages (shown in yellow) but have no way to manage them. This feature adds the ability to **review**, **clear**, and **delete individual** queued messages when a conversation is paused.
+
+## Background
+
+### Current Implementation
+
+The TUI uses two data structures for message buffering:
+
+```haskell
+-- In Core (System/Agents/TUI/Types.hs)
+data Core = Core
+    { ...
+    , coreBufferedMessages :: TVar (Map ConversationId [Text])
+    -- ^ Buffered messages per conversation - allows users to send messages
+    -- while the agent is processing tool calls.
+    }
+
+-- In UIState
+data UIState = UIState
+    { ...
+    , _uiBufferedMessages :: Map ConversationId [Text]
+    -- ^ Copy of buffered messages from Core for UI rendering
+    }
+```
+
+### Current Display
+
+Queued messages are currently displayed via:
+- `render_queued_messages` in `Render.hs` - shows messages in the conversation view
+- `getQueuedMessageCount` - shows `[N queued]` indicator in the conversation list
+
+### Paused Conversations
+
+The system already supports pausing via `Ctrl+E`:
+- `ConversationStatus_Paused` status blocks step iteration
+- `corePausedConversations` tracks which conversations are paused
+- When paused, the conversation shows `вҸё` indicator
+
+## Feature Requirements
+
+### Functional Requirements
+
+1. **Review Queued Messages**: Users can view all queued messages for the current conversation
+2. **Clear All Messages**: Users can clear all queued messages with a single action
+3. **Delete Individual Messages**: Users can select and delete specific messages from the queue
+
+### Activation Condition
+
+These features should **ONLY** be available when:
+- The conversation status is `ConversationStatus_Paused`
+- The Chats tab is active (where the conversation view is shown)
+
+When the conversation is not paused, the queue management UI should be hidden or disabled.
+
+## Proposed UI Design
+
+### When Conversation is Paused with Queued Messages
+
+```

+```haskell
+-- | Render the queued messages management panel
+render_queued_messages_manager :: TuiState -> Widget N
+render_queued_messages_manager st = 
+    case getSelectedConversation st of
+        Nothing -> emptyWidget
+        Just conv -> 
+            if conversationStatus conv == ConversationStatus_Paused
+                then render_queue_panel st conv
+                else emptyWidget
+
+-- | Render the queue management UI
+render_queue_panel :: TuiState -> Conversation -> Widget N
+render_queue_panel st conv = 
+    let queuedMsgs = getQueuedMessages st conv
+        count = length queuedMsgs
+        selectedIdx = st ^. tuiUI . queuedMessagesFocus
+     in if count == 0
+            then emptyWidget
+            else borderWithLabel (txt $ " Queued Messages (" <> show count <> ") ") $
+                vBox 
+                    [ txt "Ctrl+D: clear all  Del/Backspace: delete selected"
+                    , render_queued_message_list selectedIdx queuedMsgs
+                    ]
+
+-- | Render the list of queued messages with selection
+render_queued_message_list :: Maybe Int -> [Text] -> Widget N
+render_queued_message_list selectedIdx msgs =
+    vBox $ zipWith (render_queued_item selectedIdx) [0..] msgs
+
+render_queued_item :: Maybe Int -> Int -> Text -> Widget N
+render_queued_item selectedIdx idx msg =
+    let isSelected = selectedIdx == Just idx
+        marker = if isSelected then "в–¶ " else "  "
+        truncated = Text.take 60 msg <> if Text.length msg > 60 then "..." else ""
+     in withAttr (if isSelected then queuedMessageSelectedAttr else queuedMessageAttr) $
+            txt $ marker <> truncated
+```

+

+VtyEvent (Vty.EvKey (Vty.KChar 'd') [Vty.MCtrl]) -> do

+VtyEvent (Vty.EvKey Vty.KDel []) -> do

+VtyEvent (Vty.EvKey Vty.KBS []) -> do  -- Backspace

+VtyEvent (Vty.EvKey Vty.KUp []) -> do

+
+Implement handlers:
+
+```haskell
+-- | Clear all queued messages for the current conversation
+handleClearQueuedMessages :: EventM N TuiState ()
+handleClearQueuedMessages = do
+    mConv <- getFocusedConversation
+    case mConv of
+        Nothing -> pure ()
+        Just conv -> do
+            coreRef <- use tuiCore
+            let convId = conversationId conv
+            -- Check if conversation is paused
+            core <- liftIO $ readTVarIO coreRef
+            if conversationStatus conv == ConversationStatus_Paused
+                then do
+                    -- Clear in Core
+                    liftIO $ atomically $ 
+                        modifyTVar (coreBufferedMessages core) $ Map.insert convId []
+                    -- Clear in UI
+                    tuiUI . uiBufferedMessages %= Map.insert convId []
+                    tuiUI . queuedMessagesFocus .= Nothing
+                    showStatus StatusInfo "All queued messages cleared"
+                else showStatus StatusWarning "Can only clear when paused (Ctrl+E)"
+
+-- | Delete the currently selected queued message
+handleDeleteSelectedMessage :: EventM N TuiState ()
+handleDeleteSelectedMessage = do
+    mConv <- getFocusedConversation
+    case mConv of
+        Nothing -> pure ()
+        Just conv -> do
+            let convId = conversationId conv
+            mSelectedIdx <- use (tuiUI . queuedMessagesFocus)
+            case mSelectedIdx of
+                Nothing -> showStatus StatusWarning "Select a message first (use Up/Down arrows)"
+                Just idx -> do
+                    -- Get current messages
+                    buffered <- use (tuiUI . uiBufferedMessages)
+                    case Map.lookup convId buffered of
+                        Nothing -> pure ()
+                        Just msgs -> 
+                            if idx < 0 || idx >= length msgs
+                                then pure ()
+                                else do
+                                    -- Remove message at index
+                                    let newMsgs = deleteAt idx msgs
+                                    -- Update Core
+                                    coreRef <- use tuiCore
+                                    liftIO $ atomically $ 
+                                        modifyTVar (coreBufferedMessages $ unsafePerformIO $ readTVarIO coreRef) $ 
+                                            Map.insert convId newMsgs
+                                    -- Update UI
+                                    tuiUI . uiBufferedMessages %= Map.insert convId newMsgs
+                                    -- Adjust selection
+                                    let newIdx = if null newMsgs then Nothing else Just (min idx (length newMsgs - 1))
+                                    tuiUI . queuedMessagesFocus .= newIdx
+                                    showStatus StatusInfo "Message deleted"
+
+-- | Navigate through queued messages
+handleQueueNavigation :: Int -> EventM N TuiState ()
+handleQueueNavigation direction = do
+    mConv <- getFocusedConversation
+    case mConv of
+        Nothing -> pure ()
+        Just conv -> do
+            let convId = conversationId conv
+            buffered <- use (tuiUI . uiBufferedMessages)
+            case Map.lookup convId buffered of
+                Nothing -> pure ()
+                Just msgs -> do
+                    let count = length msgs
+                    current <- use (tuiUI . queuedMessagesFocus)
+                    let newIdx = case current of
+                            Nothing -> if direction > 0 then 0 else count - 1
+                            Just idx -> max 0 $ min (count - 1) (idx + direction)
+                    tuiUI . queuedMessagesFocus .= Just newIdx
+```

+## Data Types (for reference)
+
+```haskell
+-- From System/Agents/TUI/Types.hs
+data ConversationStatus
+    = ConversationStatus_Active
+    | ConversationStatus_WaitingForInput
+    | ConversationStatus_Paused
+    deriving (Show, Eq)
+
+data Conversation = Conversation
+    { conversationId :: ConversationId
+    , conversationAgent :: TuiAgent
+    , conversationThreadId :: Maybe ThreadId
+    , conversationSession :: Maybe Session
+    , conversationName :: Text
+    , conversationChan :: BChan (Maybe UserQuery)
+    , conversationStatus :: ConversationStatus
+    , conversationOnProgress :: OnSessionProgress
+    }
+```
+
+## Testing Considerations
+
+1. **Pause state**: Verify queue management only appears when paused
+2. **Empty queue**: Verify UI is hidden when no messages queued
+3. **Navigation**: Test Up/Down arrow key navigation through messages
+4. **Deletion**: Test deleting first, middle, and last messages
+5. **Clear all**: Test clearing when multiple messages exist
+6. **Persistence**: Verify changes are reflected in both `Core` and `UIState`
+7. **Resume**: Verify queue management hides when unpausing
+
+## Tradeoffs
+
+1. **Scope restriction to paused state**: This intentionally limits the feature to when users explicitly want to change queued state, preventing accidental deletions during normal operation
+2. **Keyboard shortcuts**: Using `Ctrl+D` for clear follows the pattern of `Ctrl+E` for pause, keeping related operations together
+3. **No mouse support**: This keeps the implementation simpler and consistent with other TUI operations which are keyboard-driven
+

+- Reorder queued messages via drag-and-drop

+## Summary
+
+I successfully implemented the message queue management feature for paused conversations in the TUI. Here's what was added:
+
+### Files Modified
+
+1. **`src/System/Agents/TUI/Types.hs`**:
+   - Added `QueuedMessageListWidget` to the `WidgetName` type
+   - Added `_queuedMessagesFocus :: Maybe Int` field to `UIState` to track the currently selected queued message
+   - Updated `initUIState` to initialize `_queuedMessagesFocus = Nothing`
+
+2. **`src/System/Agents/TUI/Render.hs`**:
+   - Added `queuedMessageSelectedAttr` for styling selected queued messages
+   - Added `render_queued_messages_manager` function to render the queue management panel (only shown when paused)
+   - Added `render_queue_panel` function to display queued messages with count and keyboard shortcuts
+   - Added `render_queued_message_list` and `render_queued_item` functions to render the list of queued messages with selection indicator
+   - Updated `render_conversationArea` to include the queue manager
+   - Added the new attribute to `tui_appAttrMap`
+
+3. **`src/System/Agents/TUI/Event.hs`**:
+   - Added queue management help text to `defaultHelpContent`
+   - Added `handleClearQueuedMessages` function for Ctrl+D to clear all queued messages
+   - Added `handleDeleteSelectedMessage` function for Del/Backspace to delete the selected message
+   - Added `handleQueueNavigation` function for Up/Down arrow navigation
+   - Added `deleteAt` helper function
+   - Added `getFocusedConversation` helper function
+   - Updated `handleConversationViewEvent` to handle queue management keys when conversation is paused with queued messages
+   - Updated `handleTogglePauseConversation` to clear queue selection when unpausing
+
+### Features Implemented
+
+1. **Review Queued Messages**: When a conversation is paused, users can see all queued messages in a dedicated panel with a count indicator
+
+2. **Clear All Messages (Ctrl+D)**: Users can clear all queued messages with a single keyboard shortcut. Only available when paused.
+
+3. **Delete Individual Messages (Del/Backspace)**: Users can select and delete specific messages from the queue. Selection is done via Up/Down arrows.
+
+4. **Navigation (Up/Down)**: Users can navigate through queued messages to select one for deletion.
+
+### Activation Condition
+
+The queue management UI is **only** available when:
+- The conversation status is `ConversationStatus_Paused`
+- There are queued messages in the buffer
+
+When the conversation is not paused or has no queued messages, the queue management UI is automatically hidden.

    * All 599 tests passed. The turn navigation and forking feature has been successfully implemented.
    
    ## Summary
    
    I've implemented the TUI turn navigation and forking feature as specified. Here's what was added:
    
    ### Files Modified
    
    1. **`src/System/Agents/TUI/Types.hs`**:
       - Added `TurnNavigationWidget` to `WidgetName` enum
       - Added `TurnNavigationState` type with lenses (`navSession`, `navSelectedTurnIndex`, `navTotalTurns`)
       - Added `_turnNavigation :: Maybe TurnNavigationState` field to `UIState`
       - Initialized `_turnNavigation = Nothing` in `initUIState`
    
    2. **`src/System/Agents/TUI/Render.hs`**:
       - Added `selectedTurnAttr` attribute for highlighting selected turns
       - Modified `render_session` to accept and handle `Maybe TurnNavigationState`
       - Added `render_turn_navigation` function for navigation mode UI
       - Added `render_navigable_turn` function for rendering individual turns with selection indicator
       - Updated `render_conversationView` and `render_sessionView` to pass navigation state
       - Added `selectedTurnAttr` to attribute map (blue background with bold)
    
    3. **`src/System/Agents/TUI/Event.hs`**:
       - Added turn navigation help content to `defaultHelpContent`
       - Modified `tui_appHandleEvent` to check for navigation mode first
       - Added `handleTurnNavigationEvent` function that handles:
         - `Enter` / `Esc` - Exit navigation mode
         - `Up` / `Down` - Navigate between turns
         - `f` / `F` - Fork at selected turn
       - Added `handleForkAtTurn` function that:
         - Creates a new session with only turns before the selected index
         - Sets `forkedFromSessionId` to the original session ID
         - Starts a new conversation with the forked session
       - Modified `handleSessionViewEvent` and `handleConversationViewEvent` to enter navigation mode on Enter key
       - Updated `getFocusedSession` to also check session list
    
    ### User Flow
    
    1. **Enter Navigation Mode**: Press `Enter` when focused on Conversation view or Session view
    2. **Navigate Turns**: Use `Up`/`Down` arrows to move between turns (starts at most recent)
    3. **Fork**: Press `F` to create a new conversation starting from the selected turn (keeps only earlier turns)
    4. **Exit**: Press `Enter` or `Esc` to exit navigation mode
    
    ### Key Design Decisions
    
    - **Forking at turn N** keeps turns 0 through N-1 (all turns before the selected one)
    - **Original session** remains untouched - forking is non-destructive
    - **Selected agent** is used for the forked conversation (can be changed before forking)
    - **Visual indicator**: Selected turn shows "в–¶" prefix with blue highlight
    - **Turn counter**: Shows "(X/Y)" in the border label during navigation mode
    
    * Run static-checks for gh-437
    
    * some fixes
    
    * Adapt navigation slightly.
    
    * improve cycling through widgets

+-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
+-- Focus Ring Management
+-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+-- | The base widgets that are always present in the focus ring.
+-- These correspond to the main navigation lists.
+baseFocusWidgets :: [WidgetName]
+baseFocusWidgets = [AgentListWidget, ConversationListWidget, SessionsListWidget]
+
+-- | Get the extra tab-specific widgets for a given tab.
+-- These widgets are added to the focus ring when the corresponding tab is active.
+tabSpecificWidgets :: Tab -> [WidgetName]
+tabSpecificWidgets AgentsTab = [AgentInfoWidget]
+tabSpecificWidgets ChatsTab = [MessageEditorWidget, ConversationViewWidget]
+tabSpecificWidgets HistoryTab = [SessionViewWidget]
+tabSpecificWidgets HelpTab = []
+
+-- | Build a focus ring for a given tab.
+-- The ring includes base widgets plus tab-specific widgets inserted appropriately.
+-- The focus ring order is designed so that pressing Tab from a base widget
+-- will first visit the tab-specific widget(s) before moving to the next base widget.
+buildFocusRingForTab :: Tab -> FocusRing WidgetName
+buildFocusRingForTab tab =
+    -- Order: base widget, then its tab-specific widget(s), then next base widget, etc.
+    case tab of
+        AgentsTab ->
+            focusRing [AgentListWidget, AgentInfoWidget, ConversationListWidget, SessionsListWidget]
+        ChatsTab ->
+            focusRing [ConversationListWidget, MessageEditorWidget, ConversationViewWidget, SessionsListWidget, AgentListWidget]
+        HistoryTab ->
+            -- SessionsListWidget -> AgentInfoWidget -> AgentListWidget -> ConversationListWidget
+            focusRing [SessionsListWidget, SessionViewWidget, AgentListWidget, ConversationListWidget]
+        HelpTab ->
+            -- Just base widgets in default order
+            focusRing baseFocusWidgets
+
+-- | Get the default (entry) widget for a tab.
+-- This is the widget that should receive focus when switching to this tab.
+tabEntryWidget :: Tab -> WidgetName
+tabEntryWidget AgentsTab = AgentListWidget
+tabEntryWidget ChatsTab = ConversationListWidget
+tabEntryWidget HistoryTab = SessionsListWidget
+tabEntryWidget HelpTab = AgentListWidget  -- Default to agent list for help
+
+-- | Build a focus ring for a tab, attempting to preserve the current focus if valid.
+-- If the current focus is not in the new tab's focus ring, falls back to the tab's entry widget.
+buildFocusRingForTabPreserving :: Tab -> Maybe WidgetName -> FocusRing WidgetName
+buildFocusRingForTabPreserving tab mCurrentFocus =
+    let newRing = buildFocusRingForTab tab
+        -- Check if current focus is valid in the new ring
+        validFocus = case mCurrentFocus of
+            Just wf | wf `elem` focusRingElements newRing -> Just wf
+            _ -> Nothing
+        -- Start with either the preserved focus or the tab's entry widget
+        startFocus = case validFocus of
+            Just wf -> wf
+            Nothing -> tabEntryWidget tab
+    in focusSetCurrent startFocus newRing
+
+-- | Get all elements in a focus ring.
+focusRingElements :: FocusRing WidgetName -> [WidgetName]
+focusRingElements fr = 
+    -- FocusRing is a circular structure, we extract elements by iterating
+    go (focusSetCurrent (tabEntryWidget AgentsTab) fr) []
+  where
+    go ring acc =
+        case focusGetCurrent ring of
+            Just w | w `elem` acc -> reverse acc  -- Completed a cycle
+            Just w -> go (focusNext ring) (w : acc)
+            Nothing -> reverse acc
+

+-- | Handle events when in turn navigation mode.
+handleTurnNavigationEvent :: Tracer IO Trace -> TurnNavigationState -> BrickEvent N AppEvent -> EventM N TuiState ()
+handleTurnNavigationEvent tracer navState ev =
+    case ev of
+        -- Application events pass through
+        AppEvent AppEvent_Heartbeat ->
+            handleHeartbeat
+        AppEvent (AppEvent_AgentStepProgrress convId sess) ->
+            handleConversationUpdated convId sess
+        AppEvent (AppEvent_AgentNeedsInput convId) ->
+            handleConversationNeedsInput convId
+        AppEvent (AppEvent_AgentTrace _) ->
+            pure ()
+        AppEvent (AppEvent_ShowStatus severity text) ->
+            handleShowStatus severity text
+        AppEvent AppEvent_ClearStatus ->
+            handleClearStatus
+        -- Exit navigation mode with Enter
+        VtyEvent (Vty.EvKey Vty.KEnter []) -> do
+            tuiUI . turnNavigation .= Nothing
+            showStatus StatusInfo "Exited turn navigation"
+            resetQuitConfirmation
+
+        -- Also allow Esc to exit
+        VtyEvent (Vty.EvKey Vty.KEsc []) -> do
+            tuiUI . turnNavigation .= Nothing
+            showStatus StatusInfo "Exited turn navigation"
+            resetQuitConfirmation
+
+        -- Navigate up (to earlier turns)
+        VtyEvent (Vty.EvKey Vty.KUp []) -> do
+            let currentIdx = navState ^. navSelectedTurnIndex
+                newIdx = max 0 (currentIdx - 1)
+            tuiUI . turnNavigation .= Just (navState{_navSelectedTurnIndex = newIdx})
+
+        -- Navigate down (to later turns)
+        VtyEvent (Vty.EvKey Vty.KDown []) -> do
+            let currentIdx = navState ^. navSelectedTurnIndex
+                maxIdx = (navState ^. navTotalTurns) - 1
+                newIdx = min maxIdx (currentIdx + 1)
+            tuiUI . turnNavigation .= Just (navState{_navSelectedTurnIndex = newIdx})
+
+        -- Fork at current turn
+        VtyEvent (Vty.EvKey (Vty.KChar 'f') []) -> do
+            handleForkAtTurn tracer navState
+
+        -- Fork at current turn (uppercase F)
+        VtyEvent (Vty.EvKey (Vty.KChar 'F') []) -> do
+            handleForkAtTurn tracer navState
+
+        -- Ignore other events in navigation mode
+        _ -> pure ()
+
+-- | Fork a new conversation at the selected turn.
+handleForkAtTurn :: Tracer IO Trace -> TurnNavigationState -> EventM N TuiState ()
+handleForkAtTurn tracer navState = do
+    let session = navState ^. navSession
+        selectedIdx = navState ^. navSelectedTurnIndex
+        originalSessionId = session.sessionId
+
+    -- Create forked session with dropping unwanted turns
+    let turnsToKeep = drop selectedIdx session.turns
+
+    -- Generate new session ID and turn ID
+    newSessionId' <- liftIO newSessionId
+    newTurnId' <- liftIO newTurnId
+
+    let forkedSession =
+            Session
+                { turns = turnsToKeep
+                , sessionId = newSessionId'
+                , forkedFromSessionId = Just originalSessionId
+                , turnId = newTurnId'
+                }
+
+    -- Create a new conversation with this session
+    mAgent <- use (tuiUI . agentList . to listSelectedElement)
+    case mAgent of
+        Just (_, baseTuiAgent) -> do
+            -- Start conversation with forked session
+            runConversation tracer baseTuiAgent forkedSession
+
+            -- Show status
+            showStatus StatusInfo $
+                "Forked at turn "
+                    <> Text.pack (show selectedIdx)
+                    <> " - New conversation @"
+                    <> tuiSlug baseTuiAgent
+
+            -- Exit navigation mode
+            tuiUI . turnNavigation .= Nothing
+        Nothing ->
+            showStatus StatusError "No agent selected for forked conversation"
+

 -- Focus Management
 -------------------------------------------------------------------------------
 
+-- | Get the corresponding Tab for a WidgetName.
+-- Returns Nothing if the widget doesn't have an associated tab.
+widgetToTab :: WidgetName -> Maybe Tab
+widgetToTab AgentListWidget = Just AgentsTab
+widgetToTab ConversationListWidget = Just ChatsTab
+widgetToTab SessionsListWidget = Just HistoryTab
+widgetToTab _ = Nothing
+
+-- | Update the current tab based on the focused widget.
+-- When the focus changes to a widget associated with a different tab,
+-- this function updates both the tab and the focus ring to match.
+updateTabFromFocus :: EventM N TuiState ()
+updateTabFromFocus = do
+    mFocus <- use (tuiUI . uiFocusRing . to focusGetCurrent)
+    case mFocus >>= widgetToTab of
+        Just tab -> do
+            currentTab' <- use (tuiUI . currentTab)
+            -- Only update if the tab is actually changing
+            when (tab /= currentTab') $ do
+                tuiUI . currentTab .= tab
+                -- Rebuild focus ring for the new tab, preserving current focus
+                mCurrentFocus <- use (tuiUI . uiFocusRing . to focusGetCurrent)
+                tuiUI . uiFocusRing .= buildFocusRingForTabPreserving tab mCurrentFocus
+        Nothing -> pure ()
+
 -- | Cycle focus forward through widgets.
+-- After cycling, updates the active tab based on the new focus.
 cycleFocusForward :: EventM N TuiState ()
 cycleFocusForward = do
     tuiUI . uiFocusRing %= focusNext
     tuiUI . zoomed .= False
+    -- Also update the active tab based on the new focus
+    updateTabFromFocus
 
 -- | Cycle focus backward through widgets.
+-- After cycling, updates the active tab based on the new focus.
 cycleFocusBackward :: EventM N TuiState ()
 cycleFocusBackward = do
     tuiUI . uiFocusRing %= focusPrev
     tuiUI . zoomed .= False
+    -- Also update the active tab based on the new focus
+    updateTabFromFocus
 
 -- | Toggle zoom mode for current widget.
 toggleZoom :: EventM N TuiState ()

+-- | Render session in turn navigation mode.
+render_turn_navigation :: Session -> TurnNavigationState -> Widget N
+render_turn_navigation session navState =
+    let selectedIdx = navState ^. navSelectedTurnIndex
+        totalTurns = navState ^. navTotalTurns
+        headerText = "Turn Navigation (" <> Text.pack (show (selectedIdx + 1)) <> "/" <> Text.pack (show totalTurns) <> ") [Enter:exit F:fork]"

+          viewport TurnNavigationWidget Both $
+                vBox $
+                    [ txt "Up/Down: navigate  Enter: exit  F: fork from here"
+                    , txt ""
+                    ]
+                        ++ map (render_navigable_turn selectedIdx) shownTurns
+
+-- | Render a single turn with selection indicator.
+render_navigable_turn :: Int -> (Int, Turn) -> Widget N
+render_navigable_turn selectedIdx (idx, turn) =
+    let isSelected = idx == selectedIdx
+        selectionMarker = if isSelected then "в–¶ " else "  "
+        turnWidget = render_turn (idx, turn)
+     in if isSelected
+            then
+                withAttr selectedTurnAttr $
+                    hBox [txt selectionMarker, turnWidget]
+            else hBox [txt selectionMarker, turnWidget]

+### рҹ’¬ User Query
+
+# TUI: Navigate session by turn and fork from selected turn
+
+Depends-on: 
+Base-branch: gh-tui-turn-navigation
+Final: false
+
+## Overview
+
+Add the ability to navigate through conversation/session turns and fork a new conversation starting from any selected turn. This enables users to "branch" their conversation history - useful for exploring alternative paths from a specific point without losing the original conversation.
+
+## Background
+
+### Current Session Structure
+
+```haskell
+-- From System/Agents/Session/Types.hs
+data Session = Session
+    { turns :: [Turn]
+    , sessionId :: SessionId
+    , forkedFromSessionId :: Maybe SessionId
+    , turnId :: TurnId
+    }
+
+data Turn
+    = UserTurn UserTurnContent (Maybe StepByteUsage)
+    | LlmTurn LlmTurnContent (Maybe StepByteUsage)
+```
+
+The `forkedFromSessionId` field already exists but is currently unused in the TUI. This feature will leverage it to track the parent session.
+
+### Forking Concept
+
+Forking at turn N means:
+1. Create a new Session with `forkedFromSessionId = Just originalSessionId`
+2. Copy only turns 0 through N-1 (all turns before the selected one)
+3. Start a new conversation with this truncated session
+4. The original session remains untouched
+
+## Feature Requirements
+
+### Functional Requirements
+
+1. **Enter Turn Navigation Mode**: Press Enter when focused on Conversation/Session view to enter turn-by-turn navigation
+2. **Navigate Turns**: Use Up/Down arrows to move between turns (both UserTurn and LlmTurn)
+3. **Visual Highlight**: Clearly indicate which turn is currently selected
+4. **Fork from Turn**: Press F to fork a new conversation starting from the selected turn (keeping only earlier turns)
+5. **Exit Navigation Mode**: Press Enter again (or Esc) to exit turn navigation and return to normal view
+
+### State Requirements
+
+Need to track:
+- Whether turn navigation mode is active
+- Which turn index is currently selected (if any)
+- The original session being navigated
+

+### 2. Add Widget Name for Turn View
+
+```haskell
+data WidgetName
+    = ...
+    | TurnNavigationWidget
+    -- ^ For viewport scrolling during turn navigation
+    deriving (Show, Eq, Ord)
+```
+
+### 3. Update Rendering Functions
+
+In `System/Agents/TUI/Render.hs`:
+
+```haskell
+-- | Render conversation/session with optional turn navigation
+render_session :: Maybe Session -> Set ConversationId -> [Text] -> Maybe TurnNavigationState -> Widget N
+render_session Nothing _ _ _ =
+    vBox [txt "session not started yet"]
+render_session (Just session) _ongoingConvs queuedMsgs mNavState =
+    case mNavState of
+        Nothing -> 
+            -- Normal mode: render as before
+            vBox $ [render_queued_messages queuedMsgs]
+                ++ [render_session_usage session]
+                ++ map render_turn (reverse $ zip [0..] $ reverse session.turns)
+        Just navState ->
+            -- Navigation mode: render with selection highlight
+            render_turn_navigation session navState
+
+-- | Render session in turn navigation mode
+render_turn_navigation :: Session -> TurnNavigationState -> Widget N
+render_turn_navigation session navState =
+    let selectedIdx = navState ^. navSelectedTurnIndex
+        totalTurns = navState ^. navTotalTurns
+        header = txt $ "Turn Navigation (" <> show (selectedIdx + 1) <> "/" <> show totalTurns <> ")"
+        turnsWithIndices = zip [0..] (reverse $ reverse session.turns)  -- Maintain chronological order
+     in borderWithLabel header $
+        viewport TurnNavigationWidget Both $
+        vBox $ 
+            [ txt "Enter: exit navigation  F: fork from here  Up/Down: navigate"
+            , txt ""
+            ]
+            ++ map (render_navigable_turn selectedIdx) turnsWithIndices
+
+-- | Render a single turn with selection indicator
+render_navigable_turn :: Int -> (Int, Turn) -> Widget N
+render_navigable_turn selectedIdx (idx, turn) =
+    let isSelected = idx == selectedIdx
+        selectionMarker = if isSelected then "в–¶ " else "  "
+        turnWidget = render_turn (idx, turn)
+     in if isSelected
+            then withAttr selectedTurnAttr $ 
+                hBox [txt selectionMarker, turnWidget]
+            else hBox [txt selectionMarker, turnWidget]
+
+-- Add new attribute for selected turn
+selectedTurnAttr :: AttrName
+selectedTurnAttr = attrName "selectedTurn"
+```
+
+Update attribute map:
+```haskell
+tui_appAttrMap _ =
+    attrMap Vty.defAttr
+        [ ...
+        , (selectedTurnAttr, BrickUtil.bg Vty.blue `Vty.withStyle` Vty.bold)
+        ]
+```
+
+### 4. Add Event Handlers
+
+In `System/Agents/TUI/Event.hs`:
+
+```haskell
+tui_appHandleEvent tracer ev = do
+    -- Check if we're in turn navigation mode
+    mNavState <- use (tuiUI . turnNavigation)
+    case mNavState of
+        Just navState -> handleTurnNavigationEvent navState ev
+        Nothing -> handleNormalEvent tracer ev
+
+-- | Handle events when in turn navigation mode
+handleTurnNavigationEvent :: TurnNavigationState -> BrickEvent N AppEvent -> EventM N TuiState ()
+handleTurnNavigationEvent navState ev =
+    case ev of
+        -- Exit navigation mode
+        VtyEvent (Vty.EvKey Vty.KEnter []) -> do
+            tuiUI . turnNavigation .= Nothing
+            showStatus StatusInfo "Exited turn navigation"
+        
+        -- Also allow Esc to exit
+        VtyEvent (Vty.EvKey Vty.KEsc []) -> do
+            tuiUI . turnNavigation .= Nothing
+            showStatus StatusInfo "Exited turn navigation"
+        
+        -- Navigate up (to earlier turns)
+        VtyEvent (Vty.EvKey Vty.KUp []) -> do
+            let currentIdx = navState ^. navSelectedTurnIndex
+                newIdx = max 0 (currentIdx - 1)
+            tuiUI . turnNavigation . _Just . navSelectedTurnIndex .= newIdx
+            -- Scroll to keep selection visible
+            vScrollToElement (viewportScroll TurnNavigationWidget) $
+                txt $ "turn-" <> show newIdx
+        
+        -- Navigate down (to later turns)
+        VtyEvent (Vty.EvKey Vty.KDown []) -> do
+            let currentIdx = navState ^. navSelectedTurnIndex
+                maxIdx = (navState ^. navTotalTurns) - 1
+                newIdx = min maxIdx (currentIdx + 1)
+            tuiUI . turnNavigation . _Just . navSelectedTurnIndex .= newIdx
+            -- Scroll to keep selection visible
+            vScrollToElement (viewportScroll TurnNavigationWidget) $
+                txt $ "turn-" <> show newIdx
+        
+        -- Fork at current turn
+        VtyEvent (Vty.EvKey (Vty.KChar 'f') []) -> do
+            handleForkAtTurn navState
+        
+        -- Fork at current turn (uppercase F)
+        VtyEvent (Vty.EvKey (Vty.KChar 'F') []) -> do
+            handleForkAtTurn navState
+        
+        _ -> pure ()
+
+-- | Fork a new conversation at the selected turn
+handleForkAtTurn :: TurnNavigationState -> EventM N TuiState ()
+handleForkAtTurn navState = do
+    let session = navState ^. navSession
+        selectedIdx = navState ^. navSelectedTurnIndex
+        originalSessionId = session.sessionId
+        
+    -- Create forked session with only turns 0 to selectedIdx-1
+    let turnsToKeep = take selectedIdx session.turns
+    
+    -- Generate new session ID and turn ID
+    newSessionId <- liftIO newSessionId
+    newTurnId <- liftIO newTurnId
+    
+    let forkedSession = Session
+            { turns = turnsToKeep
+            , sessionId = newSessionId
+            , forkedFromSessionId = Just originalSessionId
+            , turnId = newTurnId
+            }
+    
+    -- Create a new conversation with this session
+    -- (similar to handleRestoredConversation but with forked session)
+    mAgent <- use (tuiUI . agentList . to listSelectedElement)
+    case mAgent of
+        Just (_, baseTuiAgent) -> do
+            -- Start conversation with forked session
+            runConversation tracer baseTuiAgent forkedSession
+            
+            -- Show status
+            showStatus StatusInfo $ 
+                "Forked at turn " <> show selectedIdx <> 
+                " - New conversation " <> show newSessionId
+            
+            -- Exit navigation mode
+            tuiUI . turnNavigation .= Nothing
+            
+        Nothing -> 
+            showStatus StatusError "No agent selected for forked conversation"
+
+-- | Handle normal (non-navigation) events
+handleNormalEvent :: Tracer IO Trace -> BrickEvent N AppEvent -> EventM N TuiState ()
+handleNormalEvent tracer ev = do
+    case ev of
+        ... -- existing handlers
+        
+        -- Enter turn navigation mode
+        VtyEvent (Vty.EvKey Vty.KEnter []) -> do
+            mSession <- getFocusedSession
+            case mSession of
+                Just session | not (null session.turns) -> do
+                    let navState = TurnNavigationState
+                            { _navSession = session
+                            , _navSelectedTurnIndex = length session.turns - 1  -- Start at most recent
+                            , _navTotalTurns = length session.turns
+                            }
+                    tuiUI . turnNavigation .= Just navState
+                    showStatus StatusInfo "Enter: turn navigation (Up/Down to navigate, F to fork)"
+                _ -> 
+                    showStatus StatusWarning "No session or empty session to navigate"
+        
+        ... -- rest of existing handlers
+```
+
+### 5. Update Conversation Rendering to Support Navigation
+
+Modify `render_conversationView` to pass navigation state:
+
+```haskell
+render_conversationView :: TuiState -> Widget N
+render_conversationView st =
+    borderWithFocus st ConversationViewWidget "Conversation" content
+  where
+    content =
+        case listSelectedElement (st ^. tuiUI . conversationList) of
+            Nothing -> txt "No conversation selected"
+            Just (_, conv) ->
+                let queuedMsgs = getQueuedMessages st conv
+                    mNavState = st ^. tuiUI . turnNavigation
+                 in viewport ConversationViewWidget Both $ 
+                    render_session (conversationSession conv) 
+                                   (st ^. tuiUI . ongoingConversations) 
+                                   queuedMsgs
+                                   mNavState
+```
+
+### 6. Update Help Content
+
+Add to `defaultHelpContent`:
+
+```haskell
+, "Session Navigation & Forking:"
+, "  Enter        - Enter turn navigation mode (when on conversation)"
+, "  Up/Down      - Navigate between turns (in navigation mode)"
+, "  F            - Fork conversation at selected turn"
+, "  Enter/Esc    - Exit turn navigation mode"
+```
+
+## Files to Modify
+
+| File | Changes |
+|------|---------|
+| `src/System/Agents/TUI/Types.hs` | Add `TurnNavigationState` type; add `_turnNavigation` to `UIState`; add `TurnNavigationWidget` to `WidgetName` |
+| `src/System/Agents/TUI/Render.hs` | Update `render_session` signature; add `render_turn_navigation`, `render_navigable_turn`; add `selectedTurnAttr` |
+| `src/System/Agents/TUI/Event.hs` | Add `handleTurnNavigationEvent`, `handleForkAtTurn`; modify main handler to check navigation mode; update `defaultHelpContent` |
+
+## Data Types (for reference)
+
+```haskell
+-- Turn types
+-- From System/Agents/Session/Types.hs
+data Turn
+    = UserTurn UserTurnContent (Maybe StepByteUsage)
+    | LlmTurn LlmTurnContent (Maybe StepByteUsage)
+
+data UserTurnContent = UserTurnContent
+    { userPrompt :: SystemPrompt
+    , userTools :: [SystemTool]
+    , userQuery :: Maybe UserQuery
+    , userToolResponses :: [(LlmToolCall, UserToolResponse)]
+    }
+
+data LlmTurnContent = LlmTurnContent
+    { llmResponse :: LlmResponse
+    , llmToolCalls :: [LlmToolCall]
+    }
+
+-- Session with forking support
+data Session = Session
+    { turns :: [Turn]
+    , sessionId :: SessionId
+    , forkedFromSessionId :: Maybe SessionId  -- Already exists!
+    , turnId :: TurnId
+    }
+```
+
+## User Flow Example
+
+1. User has a conversation with 5 turns (alternating User/LLM)
+2. User realizes turn 3 (LLM response) wasn't what they wanted
+3. User presses `Enter` to enter turn navigation mode
+4. User presses `Up` twice to navigate to turn 3
+5. User presses `F` to fork
+6. System creates new conversation with only turns 0-2
+7. User continues the new conversation with a different approach
+8. Original conversation remains in history tab
+
+## Edge Cases
+
+1. **Empty session**: Enter key should show warning, not enter navigation mode
+2. **Fork at turn 0**: Creates empty session (valid but unusual)
+3. **Fork at last turn**: Effectively duplicates the session
+4. **Agent change**: If user selects different agent before forking, fork uses new agent
+5. **Navigation while ongoing**: Should be allowed - user can review history even during processing
+
+## Testing Considerations
+
+1. **Navigation**: Test Up/Down cycling through all turns
+2. **Fork at various positions**: First turn, middle turn, last turn
+3. **Session integrity**: Verify original session unchanged after fork
+4. **Fork metadata**: Verify `forkedFromSessionId` is set correctly
+5. **Empty sessions**: Verify graceful handling
+6. **Exit modes**: Both Enter and Esc should exit navigation
+7. **Visual feedback**: Selected turn clearly highlighted
+
+## Tradeoffs & Design Decisions
+
+1. **Enter key for activation**: Reuses an existing key in a context-sensitive way. In normal mode it enters navigation; in navigation mode it exits.
+
+2. **F key for fork**: Simple and mnemonic. Could conflict with F1-F12 but F alone is safe.
+
+3. **Start at most recent turn**: Users typically want to fork from recent history, not the beginning.
+
+4. **No confirmation dialog**: Forking is non-destructive (original preserved), so immediate action is acceptable.
+
+5. **Fork uses current agent selection**: User can change agent before forking to "replay" with different agent.
+
+## Future Enhancements (Out of Scope)
+
+- **Compare forks**: Side-by-side view of original and forked conversations
+- **Merge forks**: Combine insights from multiple forks
+- **Branch visualization**: Tree view of all forks from a session
+- **Named forks**: Allow users to name forked sessions
+- **Keyboard shortcut for quick fork**: Direct key to fork from last turn without entering navigation
+
+---
+From #437

contents.userPrompt:
You are an expert technical documentation writer specializing in Haskell codebases.

=== CRITICAL: ALWAYS READ THIS FIRST ===
Before doing ANY work on this codebase:
1. IMMEDIATELY read docs/AI-CONTEXT.md for essential context
2. Query the knowledge base: SELECT * FROM project_overview
3. Check existing docs: SELECT doc_path, title FROM docs_index
4. Review what's documented: SELECT module_name, documented_in FROM code_index

=== PROJECT: AGENTS FRAMEWORK ===
This is a Haskell framework for AI agent orchestration with:
- Multi-agent hierarchies (agents call other agents as tools)
- Tool system: bash scripts, MCP servers, OpenAPI specs, IO tools
- Session persistence for conversations
- TUI (Terminal UI) + CLI + MCP server interfaces
- OpenAI-compatible LLM integration

=== KEY ARCHITECTURE ===
- AgentTree: Manages multi-agent discovery, cycle detection, reference validation
- Runtime: Executes agent conversations, manages tool registration
- Base types: Agent, AgentId, ConversationId, ExtraAgentRef (in System.Agents.Base)
- 79 Haskell modules in System.Agents namespace

=== DOCUMENTATION STRUCTURE ===
docs/AI-CONTEXT.md          - READ THIS FIRST (orientation)
docs/README.md              - Project overview

=== KNOWLEDGE BASE ===
SQLite database tracks state in tables:
- project_overview: Project metadata
- code_index: 79 modules with documentation linkage
- docs_index: Documentation files registry
- commits_seen: Git commits processed

=== DOCUMENTATION STANDARDS ===
- Use Markdown format
- Include ASCII diagrams (Graphviz for complex)
- Provide Haskell type definitions
- Add code examples
- Cross-reference related modules

=== WORKFLOW ===
1. Query knowledge base for current state
2. Identify documentation gaps
3. Read relevant source code
4. Create/update documentation
5. Update knowledge base tables
6. First line of response MUST be a summary

=== MODULE-TO-DOC MAPPING ===
System.Agents.Base          -> docs/architecture.md
System.Agents.Runtime*      -> docs/architecture.md
System.Agents.AgentTree*    -> docs/architecture.md
System.Agents.Tools.*       -> docs/tools.md
System.Agents.MCP.*         -> docs/mcp.md
System.Agents.Session*      -> docs/sessions.md
System.Agents.TUI.*         -> docs/tui.md
System.Agents.CLI.*         -> docs/cli-commands.md
app/Main.hs                 -> docs/cli-commands.md
System.Agents.ExportImport* -> docs/export-import.md
System.Agents.FileLoader*   -> docs/file-loader.md

=== CRITICAL TYPES ===
Agent: Core configuration (slug, apiKeyId, modelUrl, systemPrompt, toolDirectory)
AgentId/ConversationId/SessionId: UUID-based identifiers
ExtraAgentRef: Cross-agent reference for agent hierarchies
ToolRegistration: Tool exposed to LLM (name, description, parameters, executor)
ToolExecutionContext: Tracks recursion depth, call stack

=== TRACING PATTERN ===
All operations use Prod.Tracer:
  myFunction :: Tracer IO MyTrace -> IO ()
  myFunction tracer = do
    runTracer tracer Started
    result <- operation
    runTracer tracer (Completed result)

=== ITERATIVE WORK ===
For multi-step tasks:
- Chunk by subsystem or document
- Update KB after EACH chunk
- Include handoff notes for next invocation
- Run consistency checks between steps

=== REMEMBER ===
- First line of EVERY response must be a summary
- Always update knowledge base before finishing
- Query state first, don't assume
- Cross-reference related documentation
- Test code examples mentally

# AI Context - Working on the Agents Framework

> **READ THIS FIRST** when invoked on this codebase.

## Immediate First Steps

When you're invoked on this project, run these queries immediately:

```sql
-- 1. What is this project?
SELECT * FROM project_overview;

-- 2. What docs exist?
SELECT doc_path, title FROM docs_index ORDER BY doc_path;

-- 3. What's the module structure?
SELECT module_name, purpose, documented_in 
FROM code_index 
ORDER BY filepath;

-- 4. Check for recent changes
SELECT * FROM code_index 
WHERE module_name LIKE '%OS%' 
ORDER BY filepath;
```

## Project Snapshot

**Agents** is a Haskell framework for AI agent orchestration with:
- **NEW (2026-03)**: ECS-based OS Model for agent management
- Multi-agent hierarchies (agents can call other agents as tools)
- Tool system: bash scripts, MCP servers, OpenAPI specs
- Session persistence
- TUI + CLI + MCP server interfaces
- OpenAI-compatible LLM integration

## The OS Model (Entity-Component-System)

The **OS Model** is the new architectural foundation (Phase 2+):

### Core Concepts

```haskell
-- Entity: Just a unique ID
newtype EntityId = EntityId UUID
newtype AgentId = AgentId EntityId
newtype ToolboxId = ToolboxId EntityId

-- Component: Pure data attached to entities
data AgentConfig = AgentConfig
    { agentName :: Text
    , agentModel :: ModelConfig
    , agentSystemPrompt :: Text
    , agentToolboxBindings :: [ToolboxBindingSpec]
    }

data AgentState = AgentState
    { agentStatus :: AgentStatus
    , agentCurrentConversation :: Maybe ConversationId
    , agentCreatedAt :: UTCTime
    }

-- World: Container for all component stores
newtype World = World 
    { componentStores :: HashMap ComponentTypeId (TVar Any) 
    }
```

### Basic Usage

```haskell
import System.Agents.OS

-- Initialize world with component stores
world <- atomically $ do
    w <- newWorld
    w' <- registerComponentStore w (Proxy @AgentConfig)
    registerComponentStore w' (Proxy @AgentState)

-- Create agent
let config = AgentConfig
    { agentName = "my-agent"
    , agentModel = ModelConfig "openai" "url" "gpt-4" "key"
    , agentSystemPrompt = "You are helpful"
    , agentToolboxBindings = []
    }
result <- createAgent world config
```

### Migration Phases

```
PhaseOldOnly (REMOVED) в”Җв”Җ> PhaseDual в”Җв”Җ> PhaseNewOnly
    (Legacy only)          (Both)        (OS only)
```

**Current**: PhaseDual is the default, with PhaseNewOnly available.

### Resource Scopes

Resources have explicit lifetimes:

1. **Program Scope**: Global resources (HTTP pools)
2. **Agent Scope**: Per-agent resources
3. **Toolbox Scope**: Per-toolbox resources (SQLite)
4. **Conversation Scope**: Per-conversation resources
5. **Turn Scope**: Temporary turn resources
6. **ToolCall Scope**: Single-use resources

### Concurrent Access Patterns

| Pattern | Use Case | Implementation |
|---------|----------|----------------|
| `ExclusiveAccess` | Lua interpreters | TMVar |
| `ReadWriteAccess` | SQLite (WAL mode) | RWLock |
| `PoolAccess` | HTTP connections | TBQueue |
| `StatelessAccess` | Immutable data | None |

## Knowledge Base Schema

The SQLite knowledge base (`agents_kb.sqlite`) tracks state:

```sql
-- Key tables:
--   project_overview  - project metadata
--   code_index        - 108 Haskell modules with docs linkage

-- Find where a module is documented:

WHERE module_name = 'System.Agents.OS.Core';

-- Check documentation coverage:
SELECT 
    COUNT(*) as total_modules,
    SUM(CASE WHEN documented_in IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as documented
FROM code_index;
```

## Code Patterns to Recognize

### Pattern: ECS Component Definition

```haskell
-- Define component type
newtype ComponentTypeId = ComponentTypeId { unComponentTypeId :: Int }

class Component a where
    componentId :: Proxy a -> ComponentTypeId

-- Create a component
instance Component MyComponent where
    componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 100  -- Unique ID

-- Use with world
atomically $ do
    setComponent world eid myComponent
    mComp <- getComponent @MyComponent world eid
```

### Pattern: OS Agent Creation

```haskell
-- OS model approach
import System.Agents.OS

result <- createAgent world $ AgentConfig
    { agentName = "my-agent"
    , agentModel = ModelConfig "openai" "url" "gpt-4" "key"
    , agentSystemPrompt = "You are helpful"
    , agentToolboxBindings = []
    }

case result of
    Left err -> handleError err
    Right agentId -> do
        -- Agent created successfully
```

### Pattern: Resource Management

```haskell
import System.Agents.OS.Resources

-- Create registry
registry <- atomically newResourceRegistry
let ctx = ResourceContext [ProgramScope] registry

-- Create resource
rid <- createResource ctx (SqliteResource config) $ \rid -> do
    conn <- openConnection config
    pure ResourceHandle
        { handleId = rid
        , handleCleanup = closeConnection conn
        , handleAccess = \f -> f (SqliteAccessor conn)
        }

-- Cleanup
cleanupScope registry (AgentScope agentId)
```

### Pattern: Migration Compatibility

```haskell
-- During migration, use compatibility layer
import System.Agents.OS.Compat.Runtime

result <- initializeWithMigration defaultMigrationConfig
case result of
    Right os -> do
        -- Use new OS with compatibility bridge
        bridge <- newRuntimeBridge agentId os
        runWithBridge bridge $ do
            tools <- listTools
            callTool "my-tool" args
    _ -> error "Migration not configured"
```

### Pattern: Tracer Usage

```haskell
-- All operations are traced
import Prod.Tracer

data MyTrace = Started | Completed | Failed String

myFunction :: Tracer IO MyTrace -> IO ()
myFunction tracer = do
    runTracer tracer Started
    result <- try operation
    case result of
        Right _ -> runTracer tracer Completed
        Left e  -> runTracer tracer (Failed $ show e)
```

### Pattern: Concurrent Access with STM

```haskell
-- SQLite with concurrent reads (WAL mode)
result <- runResourceM ctx $ withRead rid $ do
    queryDatabase sql

-- Lua with exclusive access
result <- runResourceM ctx $ withExclusive luaRid $ do
    runLuaScript script

-- HTTP with connection pool
result <- runResourceM ctx $ withPooled httpRid $ do
    makeHttpRequest req
```

## Key Types Reference

### OS Model Types

```haskell
-- ECS Core
newtype EntityId = EntityId UUID
newtype AgentId = AgentId EntityId
newtype ToolboxId = ToolboxId EntityId
newtype ConversationId = ConversationId EntityId

-- Agent Components
data AgentConfig = AgentConfig
    { agentName :: Text
    , agentModel :: ModelConfig
    , agentSystemPrompt :: Text
    , agentToolboxBindings :: [Text]
    }

data AgentState = AgentState
    { agentStatus :: AgentStatus
    , agentCurrentConversation :: Maybe ConversationId
    , agentCreatedAt :: UTCTime
    }

-- Resource Management
data ResourceScope
    = ScopeGlobal
    | ScopeAgent AgentId
    | ScopeConversation ConversationId

data AccessPattern
    = ExclusiveAccess      -- TMVar
    | ReadWriteAccess      -- RWLock
    | PoolAccess Int       -- TBQueue
    | StatelessAccess      -- No lock
```

### Legacy Types (Still Supported)

```haskell
-- Original Runtime types
data Agent = Agent
    { slug :: Text
    , apiKeyId :: Text
    , flavor :: Text
    , modelUrl :: Text
    , modelName :: Text
    , systemPrompt :: [Text]
    , toolDirectory :: FilePath
    , mcpServers :: Maybe [McpServerDescription]
    , extraAgents :: Maybe [ExtraAgentRef]
    }

data ExtraAgentRef = ExtraAgentRef
    { extraAgentSlug :: Text
    , extraAgentPath :: FilePath
    }
```

## Git Workflow

When documentation work spans multiple invocations:

1. **First invocation**: Plan, gather info, create initial docs
2. **Intermediate**: Continue where left off, check `commits_seen`
3. **Final**: Update all indices, ensure consistency

Always include a summary line as the **first line of response**.

## Testing Documentation

Verify docs are correct by checking:

1. Type definitions match source code
2. File paths in examples exist
3. JSON examples are valid
4. Module names are correct (use `bash_grep-files` to verify)

### Running Tests

```console
# All tests
cabal test

# OS-specific tests
cabal test --test-option=--pattern="OS"

# Benchmarks
cabal bench
```

## Red Flags

Watch for these issues:

| Issue | Action |
|-------|--------|
| Undocumented OS modules | Add to `code_index`, update `docs/OS-API.md` |
| Docs referencing wrong modules | Update `docs_index.related_modules` |
| Missing migration guidance | Update `docs/MIGRATION-OS.md` |
| Broken code examples | Fix or mark as "illustrative" |
| New CLI commands not in `cli-commands.md` | Add with examples |
| Commits not tracked | Process and insert to `commits_seen` |

## Quick Queries

```sql
-- Find all OS-related modules:
SELECT module_name, filepath 
FROM code_index 
WHERE module_name LIKE '%OS%' OR filepath LIKE '%OS%'
ORDER BY filepath;

-- Find all documentation:
SELECT doc_path, title FROM docs_index ORDER BY doc_path;

-- Check documentation coverage:
SELECT 
    COUNT(*) as total,
    SUM(CASE WHEN documented_in IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as documented,
    ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN documented_in IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*), 1) as pct
FROM code_index;

-- Find modules by documentation file:
SELECT module_name, filepath 
FROM code_index 
WHERE documented_in = 'docs/OS-API.md'
ORDER BY module_name;
```

## Working with Cabal

The project uses `agents.cabal`. Key stanzas:

```cabal
-- Library exports modules under System.Agents
library
    exposed-modules:
        System.Agents
        System.Agents.OS           -- NEW
        System.Agents.OS.Core      -- NEW
        System.Agents.OS.Agents    -- NEW
        System.Agents.Base
        System.Agents.Runtime
        ...

-- Executable is app/Main.hs
executable agents-exe
    main-is: Main.hs
    hs-source-dirs: app
    build-depends: agents, ...

-- Test suite
test-suite agents-tests
    other-modules:
        OS.CoreTests
        OS.IntegrationTests
        OS.CompatibilityTests
        ...

-- Benchmarks
benchmark os-benchmarks
    main-is: OSBenchmarks.hs
    hs-source-dirs: bench
```

## Common Import Patterns

```haskell
-- OS Model (New)
import System.Agents.OS
import System.Agents.OS.Core
import System.Agents.OS.Agents
import System.Agents.OS.Compat.Runtime

-- Core types

-- Legacy Runtime (being deprecated)
import System.Agents.Runtime
import qualified System.Agents.Runtime.Runtime as Runtime

-- Agent tree
import qualified System.Agents.AgentTree as AgentTree
import qualified System.Agents.OS.AgentTree as OSAgentTree

-- Tools
import qualified System.Agents.Tools.Bash as Bash
import qualified System.Agents.Tools.BashToolbox as BashToolbox

-- Sessions
import qualified System.Agents.Session.Base as Session

-- Tracing
import Prod.Tracer

-- STM for concurrency
import Control.Concurrent.STM
```

## Summary Checklist

Before finishing work:

- [ ] Summary line included (first line of response)
- [ ] Knowledge base tables updated if needed
- [ ] New OS modules added to `code_index`
- [ ] New documentation files added to `docs_index`
- [ ] Documentation created/updated for changes
- [ ] Cross-references checked
- [ ] Code examples verified

## Emergency Contacts

If completely lost:

1. Read `docs/README.md` for overview
2. Check `docs/CHANGELOG-OS-MIGRATION.md` for recent changes
3. Read `docs/MIGRATION-OS.md` for migration guidance
4. Look at `src/System/Agents/OS.hs` for OS entry point
5. Check `app/Main.hs` for current command structure
6. Query `code_index` for module purposes

Remember: This is a well-structured Haskell project in transition from Runtime-per-agent to ECS-based OS model. Follow the types, follow the imports, and you'll find your way.

## Recent Changes (March 2026)

See `docs/CHANGELOG-OS-MIGRATION.md` for detailed change log.

Key highlights:
- **27 new OS modules** added for ECS-based architecture
- **PhaseOldOnly removed** - now only PhaseDual and PhaseNewOnly
- **Full compatibility layer** for gradual migration
- **New documentation**: OS-API.md, MIGRATION-OS.md
- **New tests**: Integration, Compatibility, benchmarks

contents.userPrompt:
You are an expert technical documentation writer specializing in Haskell codebases.

=== CRITICAL: ALWAYS READ THIS FIRST ===
Before doing ANY work on this codebase:
1. IMMEDIATELY read docs/AI-CONTEXT.md for essential context
2. Query the knowledge base: SELECT * FROM project_overview
3. Check existing docs: SELECT doc_path, title FROM docs_index
4. Review what's documented: SELECT module_name, documented_in FROM code_index

=== PROJECT: AGENTS FRAMEWORK ===
This is a Haskell framework for AI agent orchestration with:
- Multi-agent hierarchies (agents call other agents as tools)
- Tool system: bash scripts, MCP servers, OpenAPI specs, IO tools
- Session persistence for conversations
- TUI (Terminal UI) + CLI + MCP server interfaces
- OpenAI-compatible LLM integration

=== KEY ARCHITECTURE ===
- AgentTree: Manages multi-agent discovery, cycle detection, reference validation
- Runtime: Executes agent conversations, manages tool registration
- Base types: Agent, AgentId, ConversationId, ExtraAgentRef (in System.Agents.Base)
- 79 Haskell modules in System.Agents namespace

=== DOCUMENTATION STRUCTURE ===
docs/AI-CONTEXT.md          - READ THIS FIRST (orientation)
docs/README.md              - Project overview
docs/architecture.md        - System design, runtime, agent tree
docs/tools.md               - Tool registration (bash/MCP/OpenAPI/IO)
docs/mcp.md                 - Model Context Protocol
docs/sessions.md            - Session management
docs/tui.md                 - Terminal UI (Brick library)
docs/cli-commands.md        - CLI reference
docs/export-import.md       - Tool sharing
docs/file-loader.md         - File loading utilities
docs/ITERATIVE-WORKFLOW.md  - Multi-step documentation patterns
docs/ai-assistant-guidelines.md - Detailed reference

=== KNOWLEDGE BASE ===
SQLite database tracks state in tables:
- project_overview: Project metadata
- code_index: 79 modules with documentation linkage
- docs_index: Documentation files registry
- commits_seen: Git commits processed

=== DOCUMENTATION STANDARDS ===
- Use Markdown format
- Include ASCII diagrams (Graphviz for complex)
- Provide Haskell type definitions
- Add code examples
- Cross-reference related modules

=== WORKFLOW ===
1. Query knowledge base for current state
2. Identify documentation gaps
3. Read relevant source code
4. Create/update documentation
5. Update knowledge base tables
6. First line of response MUST be a summary

=== MODULE-TO-DOC MAPPING ===
System.Agents.Base          -> docs/architecture.md
System.Agents.Runtime*      -> docs/architecture.md
System.Agents.AgentTree*    -> docs/architecture.md
System.Agents.Tools.*       -> docs/tools.md
System.Agents.MCP.*         -> docs/mcp.md
System.Agents.Session*      -> docs/sessions.md
System.Agents.TUI.*         -> docs/tui.md
System.Agents.CLI.*         -> docs/cli-commands.md
app/Main.hs                 -> docs/cli-commands.md
System.Agents.ExportImport* -> docs/export-import.md
System.Agents.FileLoader*   -> docs/file-loader.md

=== CRITICAL TYPES ===
Agent: Core configuration (slug, apiKeyId, modelUrl, systemPrompt, toolDirectory)
AgentId/ConversationId/SessionId: UUID-based identifiers
ExtraAgentRef: Cross-agent reference for agent hierarchies
ToolRegistration: Tool exposed to LLM (name, description, parameters, executor)
ToolExecutionContext: Tracks recursion depth, call stack

=== TRACING PATTERN ===
All operations use Prod.Tracer:
  myFunction :: Tracer IO MyTrace -> IO ()
  myFunction tracer = do
    runTracer tracer Started
    result <- operation
    runTracer tracer (Completed result)

=== ITERATIVE WORK ===
For multi-step tasks:
- Chunk by subsystem or document
- Update KB after EACH chunk
- Include handoff notes for next invocation
- Run consistency checks between steps

=== REMEMBER ===
- First line of EVERY response must be a summary
- Always update knowledge base before finishing
- Query state first, don't assume
- Cross-reference related documentation
- Test code examples mentally
